title: Analyze a valid npm package and view results
description: Verifies that entering a valid package name runs analysis and displays size stats and a visualization.

steps.description: Type "react" into the package name input field

title: Analyze a valid scoped npm package and view results
description: Verifies that a scoped package name is accepted and renders analysis results.

steps.description: Type "@types/node" into the package name input field

title: Analyze a package with an explicit version (name@version)
description: Verifies that specifying a version runs analysis and shows results for that input.

steps.description: Type "lodash@4.17.21" into the package name input field

title: Invalid package name shows 'Package not found' error
description: Verifies that analyzing a non-existent package displays the expected not-found error message.

steps.description: Type "this-package-should-not-exist-xyz-12345" into the package name input field
Verify text "Package not found" is visible
Verify element "size visualization" is not visible

title: Empty input prevents analysis and prompts user
description: Verifies that clicking Analyze with an empty input does not run analysis and shows a validation message.

steps.description: Verify text "Enter a package name" is visible
Verify element "size visualization" is not visible

title: Whitespace-only input is rejected
description: Verifies that whitespace-only input is treated as invalid and does not show results.

steps.description: Type "     " into the package name input field
Verify text "Enter a package name" is visible

title: Input with leading/trailing spaces still analyzes correctly
description: Verifies that leading/trailing spaces around a valid package name are handled and results display.

steps.description: Type "  react  " into the package name input field

title: Analyze an existing GitHub repository and view analysis results
description: Verifies a user can analyze a valid public GitHub repository and see stats and file breakdown results.

steps.description: Type "oven-sh/bun" into the owner/repo input field

title: Analyze a repository and confirm file list section is populated
description: Verifies the results include a visible files section after a successful repository analysis.

steps.description: Type "vercel/next.js" into the owner/repo input field

title: Non-existent repository shows 'Repository not found' error
description: Verifies a 404-style UI error is shown when the user analyzes a non-existent repository.

steps.description: Type "this-owner-should-not-exist-12345/this-repo-should-not-exist-12345" into the owner/repo input field
Verify text "Repository not found" is visible
Verify element "Repository stats" is not visible

title: Empty input validation prevents analysis
description: Verifies the Analyze action is blocked or prompts the user when the owner/repo field is empty.

steps.description: Verify text "Repository not found" is not visible
Verify element "Repository stats" is not visible

title: Invalid format input (missing slash) shows a user-facing error
description: Verifies the UI handles malformed repository identifiers gracefully.

steps.description: Type "invalidformat" into the owner/repo input field
Verify text "Repository not found" is visible
Verify element "Repository stats" is not visible

title: Input trimming: owner/repo with surrounding spaces still analyzes successfully
description: Verifies whitespace around the repository identifier does not prevent successful analysis.

steps.description: Type "  npm/cli  " into the owner/repo input field

title: Re-run analysis with a different repository updates visible results
description: Verifies analyzing a second repository updates the displayed repository name and keeps results visible.

steps.description: Type "npm/cli" into the owner/repo input field
Type "nodejs/node" into the owner/repo input field

title: Loading state appears while repository analysis is in progress
description: Verifies a visible loading indicator or in-progress state appears after clicking Analyze and before results render.

steps.description: Type "microsoft/TypeScript" into the owner/repo input field

title: Compare two valid npm packages and view verdict
description: Verifies that comparing two valid packages returns a side-by-side comparison and an overall verdict.

steps.description: Type "react" into the first package/repo field
Type "vue" into the second package/repo field

title: Compare two valid GitHub repositories and view verdict
description: Verifies that comparing two valid repositories shows the side-by-side comparison and verdict.

steps.description: Type "facebook/react" into the first package/repo field
Type "vuejs/core" into the second package/repo field

title: Compare with second item invalid and see partial result plus error
description: Verifies that when the second entry is not found, the UI shows an error for the second item while still showing data for the first.

steps.description: Type "react" into the first package/repo field
Type "this-package-should-not-exist-12345" into the second package/repo field
Verify text "Second item not found" is visible

title: Compare with first item invalid and see partial result plus error
description: Verifies that when the first entry is not found, the UI shows an error for the first item while still showing data for the second.

steps.description: Type "this-package-should-not-exist-54321" into the first package/repo field
Type "react" into the second package/repo field
Verify text "First item not found" is visible

title: Compare with both items invalid and see errors for both
description: Verifies that when both entries are not found, the UI shows not-found errors and does not present a misleading verdict.

steps.description: Type "this-package-should-not-exist-aaa111" into the first package/repo field
Type "this-package-should-not-exist-bbb222" into the second package/repo field

title: Attempt compare with empty inputs and verify validation message
description: Verifies that clicking Compare with missing required inputs shows a visible validation message and does not proceed to results.

steps.description: Verify text "Enter two package/repo names" is visible
Verify element "side-by-side comparison" is not visible

title: Compare with only first input filled and verify validation
description: Verifies that the UI requires both items before running a comparison.

steps.description: Type "react" into the first package/repo field
Verify text "Second item is required" is visible
Verify element "side-by-side comparison" is not visible

title: Compare after correcting an invalid second entry
description: Verifies that a user can fix an invalid entry after seeing an error and then successfully get a full comparison and verdict.

steps.description: Type "react" into the first package/repo field
Type "this-package-should-not-exist-12345" into the second package/repo field
Verify text "Second item not found" is visible
Type "vue" into the second package/repo field

title: Generate a package badge page for a valid target and see embed snippets
description: Verifies that requesting a valid badge URL shows an SVG badge and visible Markdown/HTML embed snippets.

title: Generate a repo badge page for a valid repo-like target and see embed snippets
description: Verifies that requesting a repo-style badge URL shows an SVG badge and provides Markdown/HTML snippets.

title: Badge page displays a ready-to-paste Markdown snippet that references the same badge URL
description: Verifies the Markdown snippet visibly includes the badge URL path for the currently viewed target.

title: Badge page displays a ready-to-paste HTML snippet that references the same badge URL
description: Verifies the HTML snippet visibly includes an image tag pointing to the currently viewed badge URL.

title: Invalid badge target returns an error state on the badge endpoint
description: Verifies that an invalid target produces an on-screen error message or placeholder badge indicating the target is invalid.

title: Empty or malformed badge target path shows an error state
description: Verifies that a malformed badge request results in a visible error or not-found state rather than a normal badge page.

title: Badge endpoint renders SVG output (not a JSON page) for a valid target
description: Verifies that the badge endpoint presents an SVG-style output for a valid target.

title: Badge endpoint handles special characters in the target by showing an error state
description: Verifies that a target containing unsafe/special characters does not render a normal badge and instead shows an error or not-found state.

title: Inspect a dependency inline after analyzing a package
description: Verifies that a user can analyze a package and open an inline analysis panel for a clicked dependency in the dependency tree.

steps.description: Type "lodash" into the package name input field
Click on the first visible dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency analysis section is visible

title: Switch inline analysis by clicking a different dependency
description: Verifies that clicking another dependency replaces/updates the inline analysis content accordingly.

steps.description: Type "react" into the package name input field
Click on a dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency analysis section is visible
Click on a different dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify the inline dependency analysis section is visible

title: Repeat recursive inspection by clicking a dependency inside the inline analysis
description: Verifies that the user can continue the inspection pipeline by clicking deeper dependencies and still see inline results.

steps.description: Type "express" into the package name input field
Click on a dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency analysis section is visible
Click on a dependency name shown within the inline dependency analysis section
Verify an inline dependency size visualization is visible

title: Inline analysis shows size visualization and stats for clicked dependency
description: Verifies that the inline dependency inspection displays a visualization and visible stats for the selected dependency.

steps.description: Type "axios" into the package name input field
Click on a dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency size visualization is visible

title: Dependency analysis failure shows inline error message
description: Verifies that when a dependency analysis fails, the UI shows an inline error message for that dependency.

steps.description: Type "react" into the package name input field
Click on a dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify text "Dependency analysis failed" is visible

title: Recover after a dependency analysis error by selecting another dependency
description: Verifies that after an inline dependency error, clicking a different dependency can still produce a valid inline analysis view.

steps.description: Type "react" into the package name input field
Click on a dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify text "Dependency analysis failed" is visible
Click on a different dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency analysis section is visible

title: Dependency tree remains visible while viewing inline dependency analysis
description: Verifies that the dependency list/tree remains visible for further interaction while an inline analysis is displayed.

steps.description: Type "vue" into the package name input field
Click on a dependency name in the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency analysis section is visible

title: Large dependency list: open inline analysis for a dependency after scrolling
description: Verifies that a user can scroll the dependency list/tree and still open inline analysis for a dependency item further down.

steps.description: Type "next" into the package name input field
Scroll to the bottom of the dependency list/tree
Click on a dependency name near the bottom of the dependency list/tree
Verify an inline dependency analysis section is visible
